one.zh: 婚姻无效:申请人请求宣告其 2023 年的婚姻无效,因被申请人结婚时仍与前妻有效婚姻关系（重婚）;申请未获反对,法院依《婚姻法》宣告该婚姻自始无效。
one.en: Nullity: the applicant sought a decree that her 2023 marriage was void because the respondent was still lawfully married to his former wife at the time (bigamy); the application was unopposed and the court declared the marriage void under the Marriage Act.

issue.zh: 一方在结婚时仍与他人存在有效婚姻的情况下,后一段婚姻是否自始无效、应否作出婚姻无效宣告?
issue.en: Where one party was still lawfully married to another person at the time of the marriage, is the later marriage void from the outset, such that a decree of nullity should be made?

summary.catchwords: FAMILY LAW – NULLITY – Where the applicant seeks a decree of nullity – Where the application was not opposed by the respondent – Where the respondent was married to another person at the time of the entry into the marriage with the applicant – Decree of nullity granted.

subseq.zh: 宣告为终局:该 2023 年婚姻自始无效。被申请人此后已于 2025 年末在中国办妥与前妻的离婚;双方各自承担费用,不涉子女。
subseq.en: The declaration is final: the 2023 marriage is void from the outset. The respondent has since obtained a divorce from his former wife in China in late 2025; each party bears their own costs and there are no children.

hint.en: The whole case as a story

html.zh:
申请人（Ms Faran，1970 年生，55 岁，中国公民、澳大利亚永久居民）请求宣告其与被申请人（Mr Mor，1975 年生，50 岁，中国公民）于 2023 年在悉尼 Suburb C 举行的婚姻无效。二人 2022 年 6 月相识，由获授权的婚姻主礼人依《婚姻法》主持成婚，并在出生、死亡及婚姻登记处登记;结婚证上记载申请人当时身份为「丧偶」、被申请人为「离异」。双方无子女。

关键事实:被申请人 2000 年底在中国与前妻 Ms B 结婚（育有一子）。被申请人称结婚当时并不知道自己仍是有效婚姻状态——他手持一份 2022 年 12 月、源自中国的文件，自以为那是离婚证。直到 2025 年 9 月初（因申请人为其担保境外伴侣签证而咨询中国律师时），他才被告知该文件并非中国法院作出的离婚命令。其后他在中国律师协助下申办离婚，并于 2025 年末取得中国出具的离婚证。

被申请人以其 Response 承认应作无效宣告，理由正是他 2023 年结婚时仍与 Ms B 有效婚姻关系。法院认定:被申请人结婚时确仍与 Ms B 有效婚姻;依澳大利亚法律，与他人仍有有效婚姻时再与第二人结婚并非有效婚姻，故该 2023 年婚姻自始无效，遂作出婚姻无效宣告。法院同时认定，尽管有上述背景，双方仍维持着相互间一段忠诚的终身关系。各自承担费用。
html.en:
The applicant (Ms Faran, born 1970, aged 55, a Chinese citizen and Australian permanent resident) sought a decree that her marriage to the respondent (Mr Mor, born 1975, aged 50, a Chinese citizen), solemnised in 2023 at Suburb C in Sydney, was void. The two met in June 2022 and were married by an authorised marriage celebrant under the Marriage Act, with the marriage registered at the Registry of Births, Deaths and Marriages; the marriage certificate recorded the applicant's status as 'widowed' and the respondent's as 'divorced'. There are no children.

The key fact: the respondent had married his former wife, Ms B, in China in late 2000 (one child of that relationship). He said he did not know he was still married when he wed the applicant — he held a document dated December 2022, originating in China, which he believed was a divorce certificate. Only in early September 2025 (when consulting a lawyer in China while the applicant sponsored an offshore partner visa for him) was he told the document was not a Chinese court order for divorce. He then applied for and, in late 2025, obtained a certificate of divorce from his marriage to Ms B in China.

By his Response the respondent conceded the decree, precisely because he was still married to Ms B when he married the applicant in 2023. The court found that the respondent was indeed still married to Ms B at the time; under Australian law a marriage to a second person while still lawfully married to another is not a valid marriage, so the 2023 marriage was void from the outset, and a decree of nullity was made. The court also found that, despite this background, the parties remained in a committed lifelong relationship with each other. Each party bears their own costs.

html.zh:
性质:婚姻无效宣告申请（非离婚、非财产或子女争议）。申请人依《1975 年家庭法》第 44(1A) 条有权提起婚姻无效宣告之诉;依第 51 条，该申请「应基于婚姻无效之事由」。

程序:申请人于 2025 年 11 月 28 日在 FCFCOA（第二分庭）提出 Initiating Application;2026 年 4 月 13 日案件转入第一分庭。被申请人于 2026 年 4 月 14 日提交 Response，承认该婚姻应被宣告无效。聆讯与判决同于 2026 年 4 月 17 日（口头判决，事后经整理订正）作出。
html.en:
Nature: an application for a decree of nullity (not a divorce, and not a property or children's dispute). The applicant was entitled to institute the proceeding under s 44(1A) of the Family Law Act 1975; under s 51 such an application 'shall be based on the ground that the marriage is void'.

Procedure: the applicant filed an Initiating Application in the FCFCOA (Division 2) on 28 November 2025; the matter was transferred to Division 1 on 13 April 2026. The respondent filed a Response on 14 April 2026, conceding that the marriage should be declared void. The hearing and judgment were both on 17 April 2026 (ex tempore reasons, later corrected and edited).

html.zh:
§ s51 ›
§ s44 ›
§ s23B ›
§ s5 ›
框架
婚姻无效之诉依《1975 年家庭法》与《1961 年婚姻法》:第 44(1A) 条赋予提起无效宣告之诉的资格;第 51 条规定无效宣告须基于「婚姻无效」之事由;《婚姻法》第 5(1) 条将婚姻定义为「两人排除他人、自愿缔结的终身结合」;第 23B(1)(a) 条规定，结婚时一方已与他人有合法婚姻的，该婚姻无效（重婚）。
html.en:
§ s51 ›
§ s44 ›
§ s23B ›
§ s5 ›
Framework
Nullity is governed by the Family Law Act 1975 and the Marriage Act 1961: s 44(1A) confers standing to seek a declaration of nullity; s 51 requires that a nullity application be based on the ground that the marriage is void; s 5(1) of the Marriage Act defines marriage as 'the union of 2 people to the exclusion of all others, voluntarily entered into for life'; and s 23B(1)(a) provides that a marriage is void where either party is, at the time of the marriage, lawfully married to some other person (bigamy).

html.zh: 本案未援引判例。法官径直适用《婚姻法》与《家庭法》的成文条款——婚姻的定义（《婚姻法》第 5 条）、重婚致婚姻无效（第 23B 条）、以及无效宣告之事由（《家庭法》第 51 条）——无需借助先例。
html.en: This case cites no case law. The judge applied the statutory provisions of the Marriage Act and the Family Law Act directly — the definition of marriage (Marriage Act s 5), bigamy rendering a marriage void (s 23B), and the ground for a decree of nullity (Family Law Act s 51) — without recourse to precedent.

html.zh:
1. 确认前一段婚姻有效存续 —— 法院认定被申请人与 Ms B 于 2000 年底在中国结婚。被申请人结婚时持有的 2022 年 12 月中国文件并非离婚命令;他直到 2025 年 9 月经中国律师告知方才知悉。法院接受其此前确以为已离婚的无争议证据。

2. 适用重婚无效规则 —— 依《婚姻法》第 5(1) 条，婚姻是排除他人的终身结合;第 23B(1)(a) 条规定，一方结婚时已与他人合法婚姻的，该婚姻无效。澳大利亚法律下，仍有有效婚姻时与第二人结婚不构成有效婚姻。

3. 事实认定 —— 法院认定被申请人 2023 年与申请人结婚时仍与 Ms B 有效婚姻，故该 2023 年婚姻无效。

4. 结论 —— 据此依《家庭法》第 51 条作出宣告:申请人与被申请人 2023 年于悉尼 Suburb C 的婚姻自始无效。法院并认定双方仍维持相互忠诚的终身关系。不涉费用，各自承担。
html.en:
1. Confirming the earlier marriage subsisted — the court found the respondent had married Ms B in China in late 2000. The December 2022 Chinese document the respondent held at the time of the later marriage was not an order for divorce; he learned this only in September 2025 when told by a lawyer in China. The court accepted his uncontroverted evidence that he had genuinely believed he was divorced.

2. Applying the bigamy/void rule — under s 5(1) of the Marriage Act, marriage is a union to the exclusion of all others entered into for life; s 23B(1)(a) makes a marriage void where a party was, at the time, lawfully married to another. Under Australian law a marriage to a second person while still lawfully married is not a valid marriage.

3. The finding of fact — the court found that the respondent was still married to Ms B when he married the applicant in 2023, so the 2023 marriage was void.

4. Conclusion — accordingly, a declaration was made under s 51 of the Family Law Act that the parties' 2023 marriage at Suburb C in Sydney is void from the outset. The court also found the parties remained in a committed lifelong relationship with each other. No question of costs arose; each party bears their own.

html.zh:
裁判要旨

一方在缔结婚姻时仍与他人存在有效婚姻的，后一段婚姻依《婚姻法》第 23B(1)(a) 条自始无效;法院应据《家庭法》第 51 条作出婚姻无效宣告。

当事人主观上误以为此前已离婚（例如误信一份外国文件为离婚证）并不影响后婚无效的客观结果——无效以「结婚时是否仍有有效婚姻」这一事实为准，而非以是否善意为准。
html.en:
Ratio

Where a party is still lawfully married to another at the time of a marriage, the later marriage is void from the outset under s 23B(1)(a) of the Marriage Act, and the court should make a decree of nullity under s 51 of the Family Law Act.

A party's genuine but mistaken belief that they were already divorced (for instance, mistaking a foreign document for a divorce certificate) does not change the result — voidness turns on the fact of a subsisting prior marriage at the time of the ceremony, not on good faith.

html.zh:
Decree of nullity · 婚姻无效宣告
宣告某婚姻自始无效（与离婚不同——离婚终止一段有效婚姻，无效宣告则确认婚姻从未有效成立）。
Void marriage · 无效婚姻
法律上自始不成立的婚姻;无需离婚即可宣告无效。
Bigamy · 重婚
一方在已有合法有效婚姻的情况下再次结婚;后一段婚姻无效。
Marriage celebrant · 婚姻主礼人
依《婚姻法》获授权主持婚礼的人。
Ex tempore · 当庭口头判决
法官在聆讯当场口头作出的判决（事后可经整理订正）。
Lawfully married · 合法有效婚姻
在相关法律下仍有效存续的婚姻关系。
html.en:
Decree of nullity
A declaration that a marriage is void from the outset (distinct from divorce, which ends a valid marriage; nullity confirms there never was a valid marriage).
Void marriage
A marriage that is of no legal effect from the start; it can be declared void without a divorce.
Bigamy
Marrying while already lawfully married to another person; the later marriage is void.
Marriage celebrant
A person authorised under the Marriage Act to solemnise marriages.
Ex tempore
A judgment delivered orally at the hearing (which may later be corrected and edited).
Lawfully married
A marriage that subsists and is valid under the applicable law.

html.zh:
REASONS FOR JUDGMENT

CAMPTON J:

By way of an Initiating Application filed on 28 November 2025 in the Federal Circuit and Family Court of Australia (Division 2), Ms Faran (“the applicant”) seeks a decree of nullity of her marriage to Mr Mor (“the respondent”), entered at Suburb C in Sydney NSW in 2023 as recorded in Exhibit 1.

The proceeding was transferred to the Federal Circuit and Family Court of Australia (Division 1) on 13 April 2026.

The applicant is entitled to institute proceedings seeking a declaration of nullity of the marriage pursuant to s 44(1A) of the Family Law Act 1975 (Cth) (“the Act”).

The respondent by way of his Response to Initiating Application filed on 14 April 2026 concedes the declaration that the marriage be declared void on the basis that he was still married on the same date in 2023 to his previous wife, Ms B.

BACKGROUND

The applicant was born in 1970 and is currently 55 years old. The respondent was born in 1975 and is currently 50 years old. The applicant is a citizen of China. She is a permanent resident of Australia. The respondent is a citizen of China. There are no children of the relationship.

I find that the respondent and Ms B were married in late 2000 in China. There is one child of their relationship.

The applicant and the respondent met in June 2022. Exhibit 1 concludes, and I find that they entered a marriage solemnised by an authorised marriage celebrant pursuant to the Marriage Act 1961 (Cth) (“the Marriage Act”) in 2023 at Suburb C in Sydney NSW. The marriage was registered with the Registry of Births, Deaths and Marriages in Sydney. The marriage certificate records the then conjugal status of the applicant as “widowed” and of the respondent as “divorced”.

The respondent says that at the time of the entry into the marriage in 2023, he was not aware that he was still married to Ms B. He had in his possession at the time of the marriage in 2023 what he described as a divorce certificate originating in China and dated in December 2022. He says he thought that document recorded his divorce from Ms B. I accept his uncontroversial evidence that he was told by a lawyer in China in early September 2025 that the document he had in his possession dated in December 2022 was not an Order for Divorce made in China. The engagement with the lawyer in China was the product of the respondent’s attempt to obtain an offshore partnership visa sponsored by the applicant.

The respondent says and I accept, that he thereafter applied for a divorce from his marriage with Ms B with the assistance of a lawyer in China. He gives evidence and I find that he is now “legally divorced” in China by way of a certificate of divorce issued from that forum in late 2025.

Notwithstanding the aforementioned background, I find that each of the applicant and the respondent remained in a committed lifelong relationship with each other.

THE LAW

A marriage is defined in s 5(1) of the Marriage Act as “the union of 2 people to the exclusion of all others, voluntarily entered into for life”. A marriage to a second person whilst still being lawfully married to another person is not a valid marriage under Australian law (see s 23B(1)(a) of the Marriage Act). The Marriage Act provides that a marriage is void if either of the parties are at the time of the marriage, lawfully married to some other person.

Section 51 of the Act provides that an application for a decree of nullity of a marriage “shall be based on the ground that the marriage is void”.

CONSIDERATION

I find that the respondent was married to Ms B at the time of the entry into the marriage with the applicant in 2023.

I find that the marriage between the applicant and the respondent entered at Suburb C in Sydney NSW in 2023 is void and, on that basis, a decree of nullity will be made.

Accordingly, a declaration will be made that the marriage between the applicant and respondent conducted in 2023 at Suburb C in Sydney NSW is null and void.

No question of costs arises. Each party shall bear their own costs.

For all of the abovementioned reasons, I make the orders as set out herein.

NOTE:

These reasons have been corrected from the transcript. Topic headings have been inserted, and grammatical errors have been corrected. In addition, amendments have been made to make the orally delivered reasons clear and easy to read.

CAMPTON J:

NOTE:

These reasons have been corrected from the transcript. Topic headings have been inserted, and grammatical errors have been corrected. In addition, amendments have been made to make the orally delivered reasons clear and easy to read.

desc.zh: 规定婚姻无效宣告之申请「应基于婚姻无效之事由」。
desc.en: Provides that an application for a decree of nullity of a marriage shall be based on the ground that the marriage is void.

desc.zh: 第 44(1A) 条赋予当事人提起婚姻无效宣告之诉的资格。
desc.en: Section 44(1A) confers standing to institute proceedings seeking a declaration of nullity of a marriage.

desc.zh: 规定结婚时一方已与他人合法婚姻的，该婚姻无效（第 23B(1)(a) 条）。
desc.en: Provides that a marriage is void where, at the time of the marriage, either party is lawfully married to some other person (s 23B(1)(a)).

desc.zh: 第 5(1) 条将婚姻定义为「两人排除他人、自愿缔结的终身结合」。
desc.en: Section 5(1) defines marriage as 'the union of 2 people to the exclusion of all others, voluntarily entered into for life'.

fulltext:
CAMPTON J:

By way of an Initiating Application filed on 28 November 2025 in the Federal Circuit and Family Court of Australia (Division 2), Ms Faran (“the applicant”) seeks a decree of nullity of her marriage to Mr Mor (“the respondent”), entered at Suburb C in Sydney NSW in 2023 as recorded in Exhibit 1.
The proceeding was transferred to the Federal Circuit and Family Court of Australia (Division 1) on 13 April 2026.
The applicant is entitled to institute proceedings seeking a declaration of nullity of the marriage pursuant to s 44(1A) of the Family Law Act 1975 (Cth) (“the Act”).
The respondent by way of his Response to Initiating Application filed on 14 April 2026 concedes the declaration that the marriage be declared void on the basis that he was still married on the same date in 2023 to his previous wife, Ms B.

BACKGROUND

The applicant was born in 1970 and is currently 55 years old. The respondent was born in 1975 and is currently 50 years old. The applicant is a citizen of China. She is a permanent resident of Australia. The respondent is a citizen of China. There are no children of the relationship.
I find that the respondent and Ms B were married in late 2000 in China. There is one child of their relationship.
The applicant and the respondent met in June 2022. Exhibit 1 concludes, and I find that they entered a marriage solemnised by an authorised marriage celebrant pursuant to the Marriage Act 1961 (Cth) (“the Marriage Act”) in 2023 at Suburb C in Sydney NSW. The marriage was registered with the Registry of Births, Deaths and Marriages in Sydney. The marriage certificate records the then conjugal status of the applicant as “widowed” and of the respondent as “divorced”.
The respondent says that at the time of the entry into the marriage in 2023, he was not aware that he was still married to Ms B. He had in his possession at the time of the marriage in 2023 what he described as a divorce certificate originating in China and dated in December 2022. He says he thought that document recorded his divorce from Ms B. I accept his uncontroversial evidence that he was told by a lawyer in China in early September 2025 that the document he had in his possession dated in December 2022 was not an Order for Divorce made in China. The engagement with the lawyer in China was the product of the respondent’s attempt to obtain an offshore partnership visa sponsored by the applicant.
The respondent says and I accept, that he thereafter applied for a divorce from his marriage with Ms B with the assistance of a lawyer in China. He gives evidence and I find that he is now “legally divorced” in China by way of a certificate of divorce issued from that forum in late 2025.
Notwithstanding the aforementioned background, I find that each of the applicant and the respondent remained in a committed lifelong relationship with each other.

THE LAW

A marriage is defined in s 5(1) of the Marriage Act as “the union of 2 people to the exclusion of all others, voluntarily entered into for life”. A marriage to a second person whilst still being lawfully married to another person is not a valid marriage under Australian law (see s 23B(1)(a) of the Marriage Act). The Marriage Act provides that a marriage is void if either of the parties are at the time of the marriage, lawfully married to some other person.
Section 51 of the Act provides that an application for a decree of nullity of a marriage “shall be based on the ground that the marriage is void”.

CONSIDERATION

I find that the respondent was married to Ms B at the time of the entry into the marriage with the applicant in 2023.
I find that the marriage between the applicant and the respondent entered at Suburb C in Sydney NSW in 2023 is void and, on that basis, a decree of nullity will be made.
Accordingly, a declaration will be made that the marriage between the applicant and respondent conducted in 2023 at Suburb C in Sydney NSW is null and void.
No question of costs arises. Each party shall bear their own costs.
For all of the abovementioned reasons, I make the orders as set out herein.
NOTE:

These reasons have been corrected from the transcript. Topic headings have been inserted, and grammatical errors have been corrected. In addition, amendments have been made to make the orally delivered reasons clear and easy to read.
